passages.passage_text: In such type of analogy a group of three/four inter-related words is given. The candidate is required to find out the relationship among these words and choose another group with similar relationship, from the options provided. The solved examples given below will give a better idea about this type of questions. Directions (Example Nos. 46-47) Find out the relationship among these words and choose another group with similar relationship from the options provided.

solutions.solution: (C) All are the names of rivers.
(C) 'Leap' is the movement of rabbit; 'Frisk' is the movement of 'Lamb' and 'Trot' is the movement of 'Donkey'.
(D) All are stationery goods.
(C) All are week days.
(D) They have two vowels 'a' and 'e'.
(d) None of the options is correct as they are the names of currencies.
(d) 'Jaipur' is the capital of 'Rajasthan'; 'Bengaluru' is the capital of 'Karnataka' and 'Mumbai' is the capital of 'Maharashtra'.
(C) 'Colombo' is the capital of Sri Lanka; 'Kathmandu' is the capital of 'Nepal' and 'Havana' is the capital of 'Cuba'.
(d) 'Squeak' is the sound produced by Mice; Hiss is the sound produced by Snake and Howl is the sound produced by Jackal.
(D) 'Kathak' is the classical dance of North India; 'Bharatnatyam' is the classical dance of Tamilnadu while 'Odissi' is the classical dance of Orissa.
(D) Indira and Nehru were the Prime Ministers of India, while 'Benazir' was the Prime Minister of Pakistan.
(d) All are former Indian cricketers.
(C) 'Folketing' is the parliament of 'Denmark'; 'Storting' is the parliament of 'Norway'; 'Knesset' is the parliament of Israel.
(D) They all are modes of transport.
(C) 'PTI' is an Indian news agency; 'Irna' is a news agency in Iran while 'Xin-Era' is a news agency in China.
(C) 'Pitcher' is a term used in Baseball; 'Dusra' is a term used in cricket and 'Bunker' is a term used in polo.
(d) These all are snakes.
(C) These all are units of distance.
(d) These all are water animals.
(d) All are parts of furniture.
(d) All are stationery goods.
(C) All creep and hence they come under the class of reptiles.
(d) All are cities of USA.
(C) 'Moscow' is the capital of 'Russia', 'Paris' is the capital of France and 'Athens' is the capital of Greece.
(D) All are capital cities of India. 'Mizoram' is the capital of 'Nagaland', 'Lucknow' is the capital of 'Uttar Pradesh' and 'Chennai' is the capital of Tamil Nadu.
(C) 'Kyat' is the currency of Myanmar; 'Yuan' is the currency of China and 'Baht' is the currency of Thailand.
(C) All are units of time.
(d) All represent a group. 'Crowd' is a group of people; 'Shoal' is a group of fish and 'Team' is a group of players.
(d) All are the names of parliaments. 'Duma' is the parliament of Russia; 'Majlis' is the parliament of Iran/Maldives/Malaysia and 'Khural' is the parliament of Mongolia.
(C) Young ones of given animals are called cub.
(d) All are cereals.
(C) All are games.
(D) All are insects.
(C) All are females.
(D) All work in the hospital.
(d) All are rivers.
(d) All are dress parts.
(D) All are insects.
(C) All the given names are port towns.
(d) All the given names are planets.
(D) All are the types of 'fuel'.
(C) All are headwears.
(d) All are oil seeds.
(C) All are bollywood 'Actors'.
(b) 'Table' and 'Almirah' are both 'Furniture' and similarly 'Orange' and 'Apple' are both 'Fruits'. Clearly, both second and third belong to the class denoted by the first.
(b) 'Pink' is obtained by the combination of 'Red' and 'White' and similarly 'Green' is obtained by the combination of 'Blue' and 'Yellow'.
